problems.title: Unix: Print Hello, World!
problems.instructions:
Write a simple Unix shell command (or script) that prints exactly:

Hello, World!

with a trailing newline.

{hints: Use echo with quotes; remember that the shell itself can run the command without a script file.}
problems.starterCode: # Write a Unix shell command that prints Hello, World!

problems.title: Unix: Count files in current directory
problems.starterCode: # Count regular files in the current directory

problems.title: Unix: Show current user
problems.instructions:
Print the username of the current user running the command.

{hints: The whoami command prints the effective username.}

problems.title: Unix: Show the current shell
problems.instructions:
Print the value of the SHELL environment variable, followed by a newline.

{hints: Use echo with $SHELL; environment variables are expanded by the shell.}

problems.title: Unix: Grep for a pattern
problems.instructions:
Given input text on stdin, print only the lines that contain the word error (case-sensitive).

{hints: Pipe stdin into grep 'error' or use grep 'error' without filenames.}
problems.starterCode: # Read from stdin and print lines containing the word 'error'

tests.input:
ok
error: something broke
warning
another error

problems.title: Unix: Sort unique lines
problems.instructions:
Read lines from stdin, sort them alphabetically, and remove duplicates.

{hints: The sort command and uniq work well together; sort | uniq is a common idiom.}
problems.starterCode: # Sort and deduplicate lines from stdin

problems.title: Unix: Show top 3 lines of a file
problems.instructions:
Given a file named log.txt, print only the first 3 lines of the file.

{hints: Use head -n 3 log.txt.}
problems.starterCode: # Print the first three lines of log.txt

problems.title: Unix: Count matching lines
problems.instructions:
Count how many lines in stdin contain the string TODO and print only that count.

{hints: grep -c TODO will count matching lines; use stdin instead of filenames.}

problems.title: Unix: Show disk usage for current directory
problems.instructions:
Print a single-line summary of disk usage for the current directory (the . entry).

{hints: du -sh . prints a human-readable summary; focus on invoking du correctly, not on matching exact sizes.}
problems.starterCode: # Show disk usage for the current directory

problems.title: Unix: Replace text in a file with sed
problems.instructions:
Using sed or a similar tool, read stdin and replace every occurrence of cat with dog.

{hints: sed 's/cat/dog/g' is the classic pattern; remember the g to replace all occurrences per line.}
problems.starterCode: # Replace 'cat' with 'dog' on each line from stdin
